figures.caption: Figure 1: WikiText-2 perplexity of LLaMA-2-7B under different parameter retention ratios.

Table 1: Comprehensive evaluation of pruning methods on LLaMA-2-7B. We report perplexity (WikiText-2, PTB, and C4) and zero-shot accuracy on five downstream benchmarks. Lower PPL and higher accuracy indicate better performance.
Table 2: Cross-model comparison of pruning methods at 40% parameter retention on LLaMA-7B, LLaMA-2-7B, Vicuna-7B, and LLaMA-2-13B.
Table 9: Additional evaluation on GSM8K, MMLU, BoolQ, and SciQ under different pruning ratios.
Table 16: Backbone models and their sizes used in this paper.

Table 15: Pipeline requirements of representative post-training structured pruning methods. Teacher indicates whether an additional teacher model is required beyond the pruned model itself (e.g., extra forward passes or targets from a separate checkpoint). Solver-style step indicates whether the method includes an explicit optimization/solving procedure outside standard gradient backpropagation (e.g., combinatorial optimization, closed-form or iterative solvers). Calibration data indicates whether a dedicated calibration set is needed. Backprop indicates whether gradients are backpropagated through the pruned model during optimization. Layer-wise processing indicates whether the method applies a per-layer reconstruction/solve pipeline (layers are processed largely independently), rather than a single global selection step. This table summarizes algorithmic requirements rather than wall-clock runtime, which is implementation- and system-dependent.
Table 14: Full prefill-stage memory statistics for LLaMA-2-7B under different keep ratios (bfloat16, batch size B = 4).
Table 6: Generalization to newer model families under matched pruning settings. We compare GRASPrune with FLAP on LLaMA-3.1-8B, Qwen3-8B, and Qwen3-14B at three retention ratios.
